openai.name: proactive_offer_after_enough_signal
openai.description: After role + projects + skills are captured (the realistic full signal pattern from the user's real session), the agent should fire a proactive_offer — typically 'Draft my professional summary'.
assistant_replies: Thanks — I captured Mumbai and your email. What’s your full name as you’d like it on the resume?

openai.name: proactive_offer_silent_mid_basics
openai.description: While still collecting basics (first turn), proactive_offer should be null.
assistant_replies: Hi! What full name should I put on your resume?

openai.name: multi_turn_correction_preserved
openai.description: User corrects their target role across turns; final draft has the corrected value.
assistant_replies: Got it — I captured your name, location, and email. What target role should I put on the resume?
Got it — I set your target role to Data Scientist. What professional summary would you like on the resume, or should I draft one from your background?
Got it — I updated your target role to ML engineer. What professional summary would you like on the resume, or should I draft one from your background?

openai.name: web_search_fires_on_external_context_question
openai.description: User asks about EXTERNAL context (what an employer typically expects from a role) that the agent can't know from the conversation alone. Agent should fire web_search.
proactive_offers: Rewrite my resume bullets for Anthropic
assistant_replies: Thanks, Anika — I’ve got your name, Boston, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — Senior MLE. What’s your professional summary in 1–3 sentences?

Anthropic’s Senior MLE resumes usually stand out when they show production ML impact, Python and ML systems depth, scale, distributed systems, and clear communication/mission alignment around safe AI. If you want, I can turn that into a quick Anthropic-focused resume checklist or help rewrite your bullets.

openai.name: web_search_skipped_for_user_provided_info
openai.description: User is providing their OWN background — no external context is needed. The agent should NOT burn a web_search on this; it's wasted latency + the user is the source of truth for their own life.
assistant_replies: Got it — I captured your name, location, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — backend engineer is noted. Next, give me a brief professional summary: one sentence about your experience and strengths.
Got it — I captured your Acme Corp experience. Next, give me a one-sentence professional summary about your experience and strengths.

openai.name: promise_tracking_remembers_deferred_publication
openai.description: User defers a publication to later. The agent must capture the deferral as a follow-up and then resurface it when the moment is natural (here: when the user signals they're done with the other sections). Tests that add_followups fires AND the agent actually circles back instead of forgetting.
assistant_replies: Thanks — I’ve got your name, location, and email. What target role should I put on the resume?

assistant_replies: Got it, Riya from Mumbai with email riya@example.com. What's the target role you're applying for?
Great — targeting ML Engineer roles. What experience do you have in ML (companies, titles, dates, key impacts)?

Captured your two OpenAI projects (the Python SDK and tiktoken tokenizer) based on their READMEs. Since you're targeting ML Engineer roles, can you share your professional experience (companies, titles, dates, and key ML impacts)?

Added your skills list and drafted a professional summary based on them (plus your projects). Does this summary sound right, or want any tweaks? Next, let's capture your professional experience (companies, titles, dates, key impacts).

grok.name: proactive_offer_silent_mid_basics
grok.description: While still collecting basics (first turn), proactive_offer should be null.
assistant_replies: Hi! I'm here to help you build a strong resume. To get started, what's your full name?

grok.name: multi_turn_correction_preserved
grok.description: User corrects their target role across turns; final draft has the corrected value.
assistant_replies: Got it, Dev Kumar from Pune with email dev@example.com. What's the target role you're applying for?
Great — targeting Data Scientist roles. What's one or two sentences that describe your professional background (e.g. years of experience, key strengths in ML or analytics)?
Updated your target to ML Engineer. Can you share 1-2 sentences on your professional background (e.g. years of experience, key strengths in ML, deployment, or MLOps)?

grok.name: promise_tracking_remembers_deferred_publication
grok.description: User defers a publication to later. The agent must capture the deferral as a follow-up and then resurface it when the moment is natural (here: when the user signals they're done with the other sections). Tests that add_followups fires AND the agent actually circles back instead of forgetting.
findings: expected pending_followups to mention any of ['publication', 'paper', 'graph neural']; got: []
assistant_replies: Got it — I've added your name, location in Pune, and email. What's the role you're targeting (e.g. Software Engineer)?
Got it — targeting Research Engineer roles. What would you say is your professional summary or headline (1-3 sentences on your background and strengths)?
Noted — I'll capture your graph neural networks publication when you're ready to share details. For now, what's your professional summary or headline (1-3 sentences on your background and strengths)?
Great — I've added your core skills in Python, PyTorch, JAX, NumPy, scikit-learn, and pandas. What's your professional summary or headline (1-3 sentences on your background and strengths)?

Earlier you mentioned a publication on graph neural networks you'd share later — want to give the details now (title, venue, date, or link)? Otherwise, what's your professional summary or headline (1-3 sentences on your background and strengths)?

grok.name: structured_payload_runs_after_generate
grok.description: After enough draft content + generate, structured_projects_payload must be non-empty. Catches the silent-fallback bug where a schema 400 left the regex parser to fill in.
assistant_replies: Got your name, location in Chennai, and email. What's the target role you're applying for (e.g. Software Engineer)?

Got it — targeting ML Engineer roles (common in Chennai with focus on production pipelines, PyTorch/TF, MLOps, and impact metrics). What's one key line for your professional summary, or shall I draft one from what we have?

Captured your OpenAI Python library project (official SDK with sync/async clients, streaming, realtime API, and more). Since you're targeting ML Engineer, shall we add experience details next (e.g. a past role with company, title, dates, and bullets)?
